title: ws.rs GROUP_CREATE missing rate limit — unbounded group + DB row creation
description: GROUP_CREATE handler (line 1523 ws.rs) has a subscription check but no check_rate_limit call. GROUP_ADD, GROUP_SEND, GROUP_LIST, GROUP_LEAVE all have check_rate_limit; GROUP_CREATE does not. An authenticated subscribed user can call GROUP_CREATE in a tight loop, creating one groups row + one group_members row (creator auto-added) per call with no per-minute bound. The groups table has no per-user cap. Trigger: subscribed client loops GROUP_CREATE. Bad behavior: groups table grows without bound; relay SQLite WAL balloons; prune_inactive_users eventually deletes empty groups, but only after expiry_days.
